Package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.management

Class LoggersSettings

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.rest.server.management.LoggersSettings
public class LoggersSettings extends Object
Exposure settings for the /loggers endpoint.

The GET (read) endpoints are always exposed; the PUT/POST set-level endpoints mutate runtime logging and are therefore deny-by-default on the same sensitive-by-default posture as the diagnostic dumps. A consumer opts in explicitly by registering a LoggersSettings bean (built with LoggersSettings.Builder.enableWrite()) in the resource's bean store. When no such bean is present, LoggersManager resolves the default (writes disabled) and the set-level endpoints respond 403 Forbidden.

Backend selection (explicit)

The /loggers endpoint drives a LogBackend. By default that is java.util.logging. To drive a different backend (e.g. Logback or Log4j2 via the juneau-rest-server-management-logging add-on), declare it explicitly with LoggersSettings.Builder.backend(LogBackend) — the endpoint never classpath-scans for, and then drives, a backend the operator did not choose.

See Also:
  • LoggersManager
  • LogBackend
  • Management Surface
Since:
10.0.0

  • Method Details

    • create

      public static LoggersSettings.Builder create()
      Builder creator.
      Returns:
      A new builder (writes disabled).

    • isWriteEnabled

      public boolean isWriteEnabled()
      Returns:
      true if the PUT/POST set-level endpoints are enabled.

    • getBackend

      public LogBackend getBackend()
      Returns:
      The explicitly-declared LogBackend the /loggers endpoint drives, or JulLogBackend.INSTANCE when none was declared. Never null.
